report_name: New APT 'Armored Likho' Deploys 'BusySnake' Stealer Against Energy & Government

threat_actors.name: Armored Likho
threat_actors.description: A newly discovered advanced persistent threat (APT) group conducting hybrid cyber-espionage and financially motivated campaigns against government and electric power sectors in Russia, Brazil, and Kazakhstan. The group uses the BusySnake Stealer and may leverage AI to generate payloads.

threat_actors.name: Eagle Werewolf
threat_actors.description: A threat cluster with a potential operational overlap with the Armored Likho APT group, suggesting a possible connection.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Attachment
attack_patterns.description: The primary initial access vector for Armored Likho, using malicious RAR archives in emails disguised as official government notices.

attack_patterns.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: Armored Likho's droppers retrieve second-stage payloads from public GitHub repositories to blend in with legitimate developer traffic.

attack_patterns.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
attack_patterns.description: The malware used by Armored Likho is highly obfuscated and modular to bypass dynamic analysis.

attack_patterns.name: Deobfuscate/Decode Files or Information
attack_patterns.description: The malware used by Armored Likho likely decodes its obfuscated components at runtime to evade static detection.

attack_patterns.name: Screen Capture
attack_patterns.description: The BusySnake Stealer is capable of capturing screenshots from the victim's system.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Local System
attack_patterns.description: The BusySnake Stealer exfiltrates user documents from the local system.

attack_patterns.name: Clipboard Data
attack_patterns.description: The BusySnake Stealer is capable of stealing data from the system clipboard.

attack_patterns.name: Protocol Tunneling
attack_patterns.description: The group uses the Go2Tunnel tool to create covert SSH tunnels for C2 communications, hiding traffic within an encrypted channel.

attack_patterns.name: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
attack_patterns.description: The BusySnake Stealer establishes persistence, likely using common methods such as Registry Run Keys or Startup Folder entries.

attack_patterns.name: Generative AI
attack_patterns.description: Armored Likho shows signs of using AI to generate some of its payloads, likely to create unique, non-signatured malware for each target.

malware.name: BusySnake Stealer
malware.description: A modular Python-based information stealer used by Armored Likho. It is capable of establishing persistence, exfiltrating user documents, capturing screenshots, and stealing data from the system clipboard and browser cookies.

tools.name: Go2Tunnel
tools.description: A tool used by Armored Likho to create covert SSH tunnels for command and control communications.

identities.name: Government Sector
identities.description: Government entities in Russia, Brazil, and Kazakhstan targeted by the Armored Likho APT group.

identities.name: Electric Power Industry
identities.description: Organizations in the electric power sector targeted by the Armored Likho APT group for espionage and potentially disruptive attacks.

identities.name: Defense Organizations
identities.description: Defense organizations, including those involved in UAV manufacturing, were targeted by Armored Likho.

identities.name: GitHub
identities.description: A public code repository service abused by Armored Likho for second-stage payload delivery.

infrastructure.name: GitHub Payload Hosting
infrastructure.description: Armored Likho used public GitHub repositories to host and deliver second-stage malware payloads.

infrastructure.name: Covert C2 Tunnels
infrastructure.description: The group used Go2Tunnel to create covert SSH tunnels for command and control communications.

indicators.name: Armored Likho Payload Delivery via GitHub
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Armored Likho Payload Delivery via GitHub
id: f1a2b3c4-d5e6-4f7a-8b9c-0d1e2f3a4b5c
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to raw.githubusercontent.com, which is used by Armored Likho for payload delivery. While this domain is legitimate, queries from non-developer workstations can be suspicious.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/armored-likho-targets-government.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-04
tags:
  - attack.t1105
  - attack.command_and_control
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use by developers or software updating mechanisms.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName: 'raw.githubusercontent.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects DNS queries to raw.githubusercontent.com, a domain used by Armored Likho for payload delivery. While often legitimate, queries from non-developer workstations are suspicious.

indicators.name: Suspicious Python Execution for BusySnake
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Python Execution for BusySnake
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects execution of Python processes with potentially obfuscated command-line arguments, a TTP associated with the BusySnake Stealer.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/armored-likho-targets-government.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-04
tags:
  - attack.t1059.006
  - attack.execution
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate Python scripts that use encoded arguments.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_img:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\python.exe'
      - '\pythonw.exe'
  selection_cli:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'base64'
      - 'b64decode'
      - 'exec('
  condition: all of selection_*
indicators.description: Detects execution of Python processes with command-line arguments indicative of obfuscation, a technique used by the BusySnake Stealer.

indicators.name: Suspicious Outbound SSH Tunnel
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Outbound SSH Tunneling
id: 2b3c4d5e-6f7a-4b8c-9d0e-1f2a3b4c5d6e
status: experimental
description: Detects outbound SSH network connections from workstations, which could indicate the use of tools like Go2Tunnel for C2 communications, as seen with Armored Likho.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/armored-likho-targets-government.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-04
tags:
  - attack.t1572
  - attack.command_and_control
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate remote administration or developer activity.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationPort: 22
    Initiated: 'true'
  filter_legit:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\PuTTY.exe'
      - '\ssh.exe'
      - '\WinSCP.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects outbound SSH traffic from general workstations, which may indicate the use of tools like Go2Tunnel for covert C2.
